zotero.abstractNote: 1. Automated insect identification is a critical task for biodiversity monitoring and agricultural pest management. However, the performance of deep learning models often degrades when they encounter species not present in the training set, a phenomenon known as open-set recognition. 2. We propose a deep hierarchical Bayesian learning framework that addresses the open-set recognition problem by explicitly modelling the hierarchical structure of insect taxonomy. Our approach combines a deep feature extractor with a hierarchical Bayesian model that learns to classify known species while simultaneously identifying unknown species as 'other' at different taxonomic levels. 3. We evaluate our method on a large-scale insect dataset and demonstrate that it significantly outperforms state-of-the-art open-set recognition methods in terms of both classification accuracy and the ability to detect unknown species. 4. Our framework provides a robust and scalable solution for automated insect identification in real-world scenarios where the presence of unknown species is inevitable.
